title: Build a simple in-app money sending (payments) app

requirements.text: Implement Internet Identity login and session-aware UI so users can sign in/out and the app clearly indicates the currently authenticated user.

quotes: Create a app to pay money

acceptanceCriteria: User can log in using Internet Identity and log out
UI shows a signed-in state (e.g., user principal) and a signed-out state
All payment actions are disabled or blocked when signed out

requirements.text: Create backend data models and stable storage for user accounts, balances, and a transaction ledger that records transfers between users (sender, recipient, amount, timestamp, memo, unique id).

quotes: Create a app to pay money

acceptanceCriteria: Balances persist across canister upgrades (use stable storage)
Each transfer is stored as an immutable transaction record with a unique id
Backend can return a user’s current balance and their transaction history

requirements.text: Implement backend APIs for: (1) getting the caller’s balance, (2) transferring funds from the caller to a specified recipient principal, (3) listing transactions for the caller (sent/received), and (4) (for demo purposes) optionally minting an initial balance for first-time users so they can try sending payments.

quotes: Create a app to pay money

acceptanceCriteria: Transfer rejects if amount is zero/negative, exceeds sender balance, or recipient is missing/invalid
Transfer updates sender and recipient balances atomically and writes exactly one transaction record
Transaction listing returns deterministic, paginated or capped results to avoid unbounded responses
New users can obtain a small initial balance in a controlled way (e.g., one-time grant) to enable testing

requirements.text: Build the core payment UI: a ‘Send payment’ form that lets a signed-in user enter recipient principal, amount, and an optional memo, then submit and receive clear success/error feedback.

quotes: Create a app to pay money

acceptanceCriteria: Form validates recipient principal format and numeric amount before submission
On success, UI shows a confirmation including amount and recipient
On failure, UI shows a human-readable error message returned/mapped from the backend
After a successful send, balance and history views refresh automatically

requirements.text: Add a dashboard screen showing the signed-in user’s current balance and a transaction history list with sent vs received entries (amount, counterparty, timestamp, memo).

quotes: Create a app to pay money

acceptanceCriteria: Balance is loaded from the backend and displayed prominently
Transaction history loads from the backend and distinguishes sent vs received
History handles empty state and loading/error states cleanly

requirements.text: Add basic security/abuse controls in the backend: enforce that only the caller can spend the caller’s balance, and rate-limit or otherwise constrain any demo mint/initial-balance feature to prevent repeated farming by the same principal.

quotes: Create a app to pay money

acceptanceCriteria: No API allows spending from an account other than the caller’s account
Initial balance/minting (if provided) can be claimed at most once per principal (or equivalent constraint)
Backend returns consistent error variants/messages for unauthorized/invalid actions

requirements.text: Create a coherent visual theme for a finance/payments app (colors, typography, spacing, component styling) and apply it consistently across all screens without using a blue/purple primary palette.

quotes: Create a app to pay money

acceptanceCriteria: App has a consistent non-blue/non-purple primary color and a secondary accent
All main UI (header, cards, forms, lists, buttons) follows the chosen theme
Theme is applied consistently across signed-out and signed-in states

requirements.text: Add generated static image assets (logo and a simple hero/background illustration) under frontend/public/assets/generated and use them in the UI (e.g., header/logo area and signed-out landing state).

quotes: Create a app to pay money

acceptanceCriteria: Images exist in frontend/public/assets/generated with the specified filenames
UI renders the logo in the app header (or equivalent prominent location)
UI renders the hero/background illustration on the signed-out landing state without loading from the backend

constraints: Backend must remain a single Motoko actor in backend/main.mo (no additional backend services)
Do not modify any files under frontend immutablePaths; compose existing hooks/components instead
Use Internet Identity only for authentication (no third-party auth providers)
Do not integrate external databases or external payment providers
nonGoals: Connecting to real-world bank accounts or card payments
Using external ledgers or external token standards beyond an in-canister balance model
Real-time chat or WebSocket-based live updates

required: Minimalist payments app logo, flat vector style, dark-on-light (paymark-logo.dim_512x512.png)
Abstract finance-themed hero illustration with shapes and subtle texture, light background, warm accent colors (payments-hero.dim_1600x900.png)

goalsToAdd: Build a simple app that lets users send payments to each other (in-app balance)
